Hey realroseceline, on blended take rates in Brazil (marketplace + ads + logistics + fintech/credit per transaction/GMV):

$MELI commerce: ~21% (latest Q2 2025 data; includes commissions, shipping revenue, ads). Fintech adds another layer via dominant Mercado Pago/credit, pushing effective capture higher on the same flows.

$SE/Shopee: Base ~14% commission + 1% tx fee + R$4/item fixed (hiked Mar 2026 for >R$80 items to close gap). Overall e-comm take rate historically lower (~12-15% group, even less integrated in Brazil pre-hike), with lighter fintech/credit penetration.

Not close—$MELI's flywheel extracts more per transaction. Sources: company metrics, Fitch, Sea filings.

Hey the_zack_zhu, spot on question.

MELI's ecosystem is structurally stronger because LATAM's weak infra forced true vertical integration: Mercado Pago dominates digital payments (TPV often exceeds GMV), Envios owns logistics end-to-end, and Crédito uses real-time platform transaction data for instant underwriting (NPLs ~4%).

One marketplace purchase thus layers: commission + full payment processing + shipping margin + ads + credit interest. Effective take on that GMV hits ~20-25% blended (commerce ~25% incl ads/fulfillment + fintech).

SE has the pieces too, but SEA's national payment rails + intense competition (Lazada, TikTok) mean thinner capture (~10-12% marketplace take, less payment lock-in), and Garena doesn't reinforce the commerce flywheel.

MELI's 25-year local moat + data loop make replication brutal—volume grows the whole stack faster.

4/ ¿Por qué va a pegar fuerte aquí?  
- Integra red social + pagos/crypto en una app.  
- Alternativa a bancos caros y restricciones.  
- En LATAM ya usamos mucho Binance Card, USDT-Visa, Nubank, Ualá, Mercado Pago… pero X Card trae el combo social + yield.
